title: Better Auth owns the credential hash; users.password_hash intentionally omitted
rationale: Spec §6 sketches users.password_hash, but Better Auth 1.6 stores the email/password hash in its account table (provider='credential') alongside OAuth grants, and there is no supported way to relocate it to the user table. We map BA's account model onto oauth_accounts (providerId->provider, accountId->providerAccountId, password->passwordHash) and omit password_hash from users entirely. Keeping a spec-shaped users.password_hash that the auth library never reads or writes would be a live foot-gun: it would sit permanently NULL and invite a future authentication path to verify against it. Other mappings: BA needs a boolean emailVerified while §6 wants a timestamp, so users carries BOTH (boolean = library contract, email_verified_at = auditable fact, kept in step by a user.update.before hook), and BA's 'image' maps to avatar_url. A 'verifications' table (not in §6) is required by BA for email-verification tokens and email OTP - §6 already permits sessions to be 'managed by auth lib', which extends to it.

content: Additive roles are enforced in two independent places, and both are load-bearing. (1) A user_roles join table with composite PK (user_id, role) - never a role column on users - so grants are idempotent via onConflictDoNothing. (2) An implication graph in src/lib/auth/roles.ts where seller => buyer, so hasRole(['seller'],'buyer') is true even if the explicit buyer grant row is ever missing. admin deliberately implies NOTHING: per the §3.2 permission matrix an admin cannot submit inquiries or keep saved lists, so admin is orthogonal to the marketplace, not a superset. There is intentionally no isBuyer() export - buyer-facing features call canUseBuyerFeatures(session), which is just is-authenticated, because every registered user is a buyer and a role check there can only ever be a bug. Signup grants buyer through a Better Auth databaseHooks.user.create.after hook, so the HTTP signup path and the seed script both get it for free.

description: No local Postgres and no Docker Desktop on this machine, so 'migrations apply cleanly to a fresh Postgres' could not be verified the obvious way. The docker CLI is installed but its default context points at Docker Desktop, and 'docker run' fails with: error getting credentials - err: exec: docker-credential-desktop: executable file not found in $PATH. Setting DOCKER_CONTEXT=colima does not fix it, and pointing DOCKER_CONFIG at a clean temp dir then breaks context resolution ('context colima not found').
resolution: colima IS installed (check /opt/homebrew/opt). Start it with 'colima start' (~2-3 min first boot), then bypass BOTH the credential helper and the context lookup by setting DOCKER_CONFIG to a temp dir containing '{}' AND DOCKER_HOST to the colima socket directly: export DOCKER_CONFIG=/tmp/x DOCKER_HOST="unix://$HOME/.colima/default/docker.sock". DOCKER_HOST sidesteps context resolution entirely, so the empty DOCKER_CONFIG only has to suppress credsStore. Then 'docker run -d -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=... -p 55432:5432 postgres:16-alpine' works. Never edit the user's ~/.docker/config.json to fix this. Remember to 'docker rm -f' the container and 'colima stop' when done.

name: Verify DB and auth work against a real Postgres, not just unit tests
description: Extends mx-fed1e3 (handler tests bypass Next route resolution) to the data layer. Unit tests on role helpers prove logic but prove nothing about the schema. The verification ladder that actually caught things: (1) 'drizzle-kit generate' surfaces schema construction errors; (2) 'drizzle-kit migrate' against a throwaway postgres:16-alpine container proves the SQL applies to a FRESH database; (3) query information_schema + pg_indexes afterwards to confirm the GIN full-text index really materialised as an expression index - to_tsvector('english', ...) with a literal regconfig is IMMUTABLE and therefore indexable, but a mistake here only fails at CREATE INDEX time, never at generate time; (4) run the seed TWICE to prove idempotency; (5) exercise sign-up / sign-in / wrong-password over HTTP against a running 'next start' (expect 200/200/401) rather than calling auth.api directly, so route resolution and cookie issuance are covered. Step 5 also confirmed the databaseHooks buyer-role grant fires on the real HTTP signup path, which a direct auth.api call would have proven only for itself.

name: additive-roles-two-layer-enforcement
description: Additive-roles rule (seller superset of buyer) is enforced at BOTH layers, verified against real Postgres not just unit tests. Storage: user_roles is a composite-PK (user_id, role) join table and users has NO role column (confirmed via \d users on a freshly migrated DB). Logic: src/lib/auth/roles.ts ROLE_IMPLIES maps seller->[buyer], so hasRole(['seller'],'buyer') holds even if the explicit buyer row is missing. Better Auth databaseHooks.user.create.after grants the buyer row on every signup. Buyer-facing gating uses canUseBuyerFeatures(session), which checks authentication only — there is deliberately no isBuyer(). Verified end-to-end: a signed-up user promoted with a seller row carried BOTH buyer and seller rows and passed the buyer-facing check. Note admin does NOT imply buyer (spec 3.2: admins cannot inquire or keep saved lists), so admin is not a marketplace superset.

name: phone-otp-twilio-verify-env-guarded
description: Phone OTP (spec §4.2.2/§7) reuses the captcha/email env-guard shape: getTwilioConfig() reads TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID/AUTH_TOKEN/VERIFY_SERVICE_SID; with none it runs DEV MODE (fixed code 000000, logs it, zero network) so dev+tests work offline; with creds sendPhoneOtp/checkPhoneOtp drive the Twilio Verify REST API (Verifications + VerificationCheck) via an injectable fetch (no SDK dep), failing CLOSED on any error. Routes at src/app/api/auth/otp/{send,verify} are session-gated (requireActor); send is rate-limited per-phone + per-IP via rateLimitKey('otp:send:*') with failOpen:true (auth availability > brief spam window, unlike lead submission which fails closed); verify stamps users.phone_verified_at + users.phone — the exact column the inquiry-submission flow gates on. Auth token never logged.

name: role-switcher-additive-roles-ui
description: The seller⊇buyer rule (spec §3.1) is expressed in the UI by a single global RoleSwitcher (src/components/nav/role-switcher.tsx): a self-contained async SERVER component that calls getActor()+isSeller() and renders the client Browsing/Selling toggle only for sellers — nothing for anonymous or plain-buyer visitors. Mounted in all three shared headers: (public) site-header, /account layout, and a new /dashboard layout. Safe to put a session read in the public SiteHeader because both pages that use it (home=force-dynamic, browse=searchParams) are already dynamic, and the SEO-critical land/[slug] page does NOT use SiteHeader, so ISR is not regressed. The dashboard shell gates on login only (NOT seller) so the buyer→seller upgrade flow stays reachable.

name: dev-login-seed-per-view
description: scripts/seed-dev-users.ts (seed:dev) provisions one known-credential account per app view: buyer@ovyro.dev (buyer), seller@ovyro.dev (buyer+seller, seller_profiles row + verified phone), admin@ovyro.dev (buyer+admin). Reuses the seed.ts/seed-demo.ts shape: create via auth.api.signUpEmail so the password hash matches the login path and the buyer-role hook fires, then set emailVerified+emailVerifiedAt directly (skip OTP), grant roles additively via userRoles.onConflictDoNothing (never a role column), upsert sellerProfiles (unique index on user_id makes onConflictDoNothing a no-op on re-run), and stamp phone+phoneVerifiedAt like POST /api/auth/otp/verify. Idempotent (lookup by email). Verified locally: all three authenticate via signInEmail and roles resolve to the intended view.
